resposta:
solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def findMaxK(self, nums: List[int]) -> int:
        nums_set = set(nums)
        max_k = -1
        for num in nums_set:
            if -num in nums_set and abs(num) > max_k:
                max_k = abs(num)
        return max_k
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(N)
space complexity: O(N)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution utilizes a set to efficiently check for the existence of negative counterparts. It iterates through the set, comparing each element with its negative and updating the maximum k accordingly.  The set provides O(1) lookup time, resulting in a linear time complexity.  The space complexity is also linear due to the set storing unique elements.
